questions.q: What does the term 'rake' refer to in casino and poker contexts?
choices: The angle at which dealers arrange chips on the table
The slope of the craps table
A tool used to manage the roulette wheel
The commission or percentage the house takes from pots or wagers

questions.q: What dice game has its origins traced to the Crusades and is a casino classic?

source.notes: Derived from 'hazard,' an English game played in the Crusades.

questions.q: What is the magic number you try to reach (without going over) in blackjack?

source.notes: Going over 21 is 'busting' — automatic loss. audit-corrected 2026-05-09: removed the ambiguous 'Closest to 21' distractor (which is also technically correct) and reworded stem to ask for the target number.

questions.q: What's the highest hand in 5-card poker?
How many numbers are on an American roulette wheel (with 0 and 00)?
What does the green '0' or '00' mean for the player on roulette?
choices: House always wins on these — they're the casino's edge

source.notes: The 0/00 are why even-money bets like red/black actually pay <50%.

questions.q: What's the name of the iconic Las Vegas Strip's 'fountain show' resort?
What casino has the iconic 'Welcome to Fabulous Las Vegas' sign as its neighbor?
choices: The sign is technically in front of all of South Strip — Mandalay Bay is closest

source.notes: Designed by Betty Willis in 1959. Located on the median south of Mandalay Bay.

questions.q: What's blackjack's other common name?
choices: Both — 21 is the US name; Pontoon is the British variant

source.notes: All trace back to French Vingt-et-un ('twenty-one').

questions.q: What term specifically describes a player on Nevada's formal name-by-name casino exclusion list?

source.notes: Nevada's Black Book (formally the List of Excluded Persons) is the state's formal exclusion list since 1960. audit-corrected 2026-05-09: replaced ambiguous '86'd' / 'All accepted' choices and tightened stem so 'In the Black Book' is uniquely correct.

questions.q: What's the legal age for most European casinos?

source.notes: Higher in some venues by house policy.

questions.q: Which casino game is famous for the strategy of 'card counting'?

source.notes: Casinos can ban suspected counters, but counting is not illegal.

questions.q: Which 1995 Martin Scorsese film stars De Niro, Pesci, and Stone in 1970s Vegas?

source.notes: Loosely based on the real-life Frank Rosenthal.

questions.q: Which iconic Las Vegas hotel hosted Frank Sinatra and the Rat Pack regularly?

source.notes: Demolished 1996. Site is now The Venetian.
